config.title: ⚙️ Settings
config.subtitle: Twitch connection, window preferences, and about
config.twitchDesc: Connect your Twitch channel to enable live chat, giveaways, and predictions.
config.conectaTwitch: Connect with Twitch to use all features
config.loginNavegadorTitle: Open login in browser (safe alternative while streaming)
config.authInstrucciones: Authorize the app and come back here
config.infoEventsub: 💡 If you see 403 errors in the console about EventSub, disconnect and reconnect Twitch to renew permissions with the latest scopes.
config.appDesc: Modular open-source application for content creators. Manage your Twitch channel with custom overlays, interactive giveaways, predictions, and real-time chat.
config.licencia: AGPLv3 License · Commercial use requires dual license
config.apoyoDesc: StreamForger is open-source and free. If you like the project, consider buying me a coffee.

obs.startDesc: Neutral starting soon screen with timer, progress bar and custom logo support (?logo=URL parameter)
obs.chatOverlayDesc: Real-time chat messages with themed animations

security.title: Anti-Bot Protection
security.subtitle: Automatic bot and spam detection inspired by Sery Bot
security.followBotsDesc: Automatically detect and block suspicious accounts when they follow the channel
security.spamFilterDesc: Detects messages with shortened links, unsolicited promotions and spam patterns
security.autoBanDesc: Automatically ban users detected as bots. If disabled, they will only be flagged
security.scanDesc: Check the current follower list for suspicious patterns
security.helpText: 🧠 The Anti-Bot system analyzes patterns in usernames and chat messages to detect suspicious accounts. Follow bots are detected automatically when they follow the channel. The spam filter checks messages for shortened links and promotions. Enable Auto-ban to block automatically, or review detections and use the manual buttons. Use the whitelist to exclude legitimate users.

tracker.title: 📊 Twitch Tracker
tracker.subtitle: Your channel's performance statistics
tracker.error: Could not load statistics. Make sure Twitch is connected.
tracker.empty: No archived streams in this period.

giveaway.title: 🎁 Giveaways
giveaway.subtitle: Manage giveaways with ticket system, subscription multiplier and random wheel
giveaway.configText1: Create a Twitch channel points reward called
giveaway.configText4: When a viewer redeems it during an active giveaway, they'll receive extra tickets automatically (1 ticket per 1000 pts redeemed).
giveaway.subMultiplierDesc: Subscribers get extra tickets automatically when typing
giveaway.subMultiplierDetail: : Tier 1 = ×2, Tier 2 = ×5, Tier 3 = ×10 tickets. Non-subscribers get 1 base ticket.
giveaway.puntosDesc: Viewers can redeem "{{reward}}" for {{cost}} pts to get extra tickets
giveaway.puntosDesactivado: Disabled — only the !giveaway command will be used
giveaway.emptyChannel: Enter your Twitch channel in the top bar
giveaway.ruletaDesc: Add names and spin the wheel to pick a random winner.
giveaway.importarPlaceholder: Paste names here, separated by commas or one per line
giveaway.nombresHint: Names separated by commas or line breaks

commands.varUser: Name of the user who ran the command
commands.varUses: Times the command has been used
commands.title: 🤖 Commands
commands.subtitle: Manage your chat's custom commands
commands.variablesHelp: Type { in the response to see the variable menu. You can also use them directly:
commands.empty: No commands configured. Create your first one above.
commands.emptyChannel: Connect a channel to manage commands.

subathon.title: 🔴 Subathon
subathon.subtitle: Extendable timer for live streams. Viewers add time with subs, bits, or rewards.
subathon.alertsDesc: Display animated pop-ups when time is added. Keeps the energy high!
subathon.designDesc: Customize colors and fonts to match your brand.

timer.title: ⏱️ Timer
timer.subtitle: Live countdown for the stream. Appears as an OBS overlay.

hud.subtitle: Live channel statistics. Appears as an automatic OBS overlay.
hud.empty: Connect a channel to see statistics.
hud.autoDesc: HUD updates every 10 seconds via Socket.IO when enabled.

scoreboard.title: ⚔️ Fighter Scoreboard
scoreboard.subtitle: Fighting game overlay with health bars, rounds and timer.

mod.title: 🛡️ Moderation

predictions.preguntaPlaceholder: e.g. Will we beat the boss this attempt?
predictions.emptyChannel: Enter your Twitch channel in the top bar to start

overlay.clickThroughOn: Enable click-through (clicks pass to the game)
overlay.arrastra: Drag from the top edge to move

preview.title: 📺 Preview
preview.subtitle: Preview your Twitch channel directly from the app
preview.emptyState: Enter a Twitch channel in the top bar to see the preview
preview.tip: 💡 Use the Twitch embedded player to verify your stream is reaching OBS correctly. Make sure localhost, 127.0.0.1 and {{hostname}} are added as allowed domains in the Twitch Developer Console.

activity.conectar: Connect a channel to see activity.

info.title: ✏️ Stream Info

bitrate.title: 🧮 Bitrate Calculator
bitrate.subtitle: Calculate the optimal bitrate for your Twitch stream
bitrate.bppDesc: 0.08-0.10 for slow games, 0.10-0.15 for fast-paced games with lots of movement
bitrate.usageDesc: Use 80-90% of your upload to leave headroom for other devices
bitrate.speedDesc: Enter your upload speed to get an automatic configuration recommendation
bitrate.safeBitrateResult: Max safe bitrate: {bitrate} kbps (80% of your upload)
bitrate.recommendationDesc: Based on your upload speed, this is the best configuration for your stream:
bitrate.connectionOk: ✅ Your connection supports this configuration ({needed} / {upload} Mbps)
bitrate.connectionWarn: ⚠️ This configuration needs {needed} Mbps but your upload is {upload} Mbps. Reduce resolution, FPS or BPP.
bitrate.obsGuideForSpeed: 📡 Configuration for your {speed} Mbps connection: use {label} — values are already calculated above
bitrate.warning: ⚠️ Total bitrate exceeds Twitch's recommended 6000 kbps. Reduce resolution, FPS, or BPP.
bitrate.recommendation: ✅ Bitrate is within Twitch's limit (6000 kbps).
bitrate.aboutText: Bitrate is the amount of data you send to Twitch per second. It depends on resolution, FPS, and visual quality (BPP). Too low bitrate causes pixelation, too high causes buffering for viewers. Twitch recommends a maximum of 6000 kbps for affiliates and 8500 kbps for partners. Adjust these values based on your upload connection and content type.
bitrate.obsGuideDesc: Apply these values in OBS Studio for an optimal stream. Go to Settings → Output.
bitrate.obsGuideAudioDesc: In Settings → Audio, make sure you have:
bitrate.obsGuideOutputStep: In Simple mode, just set the Video bitrate. In Advanced mode (recommended), configure:

achievements.title: 🏆 Achievements
achievements.subtitle: Track your progress toward Twitch Affiliate and Partner
achievements.noData: No achievement data available. Make sure Twitch is connected.
achievements.authHint: Connect Twitch in Settings to view your achievements.
achievements.apiNote: Twitch does not expose all achievement data via API. Click below to see your full progress on Twitch.
